page: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-4 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated physical cores with 4 vCPUs, operating at 1.0 thread per core. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Cache resources include 32 KB of L1 data cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU execution, with stress-ng single-core performance ranking in the top 25%. Multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench scores, and Passmark CPU metrics fall within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests show average throughput, reaching 498.39 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 48.77 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and static web serving throughput are relatively weak, placing in the bottom 25% of benchmarked systems.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and balanced resource profile, the t2d-standard-4 is suited for general-purpose workloads such as database operations, software compilation, and lightweight text or image processing. It also supports basic large language model inference for small models, achieving 202.5 tokens per second for text generation on a 135M parameter model. The absence of bundled local storage and complimentary public IPv4 addresses represents a tradeoff, requiring external storage attachments for persistent data. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated AMD EPYC cores deliver reliable, non-burstable compute performance for standard business applications without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware.
description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-4 is a general-purpose server instance configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, utilizing the x86_64 AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. Operating with 4 physical cores and a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, it delivers a balanced resource profile without bundled local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data highlights strong single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25%, while multi-core execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform in the average tier. Conversely, static web serving throughput and multi-threaded decompression show weaker relative performance. This instance is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, including software compilation, database operations, and small-scale language model inference, offering a cost-efficient, non-burstable compute option for standard enterprise applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-4 is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and strong single-core CPU performance for standard enterprise workloads.

tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC instance offering balanced compute and memory resources with strong single-core performance for general-purpose workloads.

Balanced 16.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core
Strong single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
No bundled local storage or integrated GPU accelerators
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and small LLM inference
